name: Google
philosophy: Google's web presence is the canonical Material You / Material 3 expression — paper-bright canvas, friendly geometric type (Google Sans + Roboto), saturated four-color brand palette, generous radii (16–28px), and a card-based layout grammar that treats every product feature as a small bento tile. The atmosphere is approachable, optimistic, and consumer-grade — even on enterprise Cloud pages.

design_language.motion_signature: Material motion — emphasized easing curves (cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1)), 200–300ms transition baseline, container-transform morphs between states. The signature is the spring-easing emphasized curve that makes Material interactions feel grounded rather than flat.

trademark_signals: Google's four brand colors (Blue #4285f4, Red #ea4335, Yellow #fbbc04, Green #34a853) used as semantic + decorative chromatic system
Google Sans (display + body) — proprietary geometric humanist sans with friendly rounded terminals
Generous radii — cards at 16–28px, buttons at 20–28px pill or 8–12px rounded-rect. The brand reads as soft and approachable
Bento-tile layouts — small feature cards in 3-up or 4-up grids, each with an illustration + headline + short description
Material 3 surface tonal elevation — surfaces shift hue subtly with elevation rather than relying on shadow
ai_slop_avoided: Don't use the four brand colors as decorative gradients — they are semantic and brand-mark only
Don't replace Google Sans with Roboto for display sizes — the display variant is part of the brand
Don't sharpen card corners below 8px radius — the soft radii are the brand's friendliness signal
Don't add deep drop shadows — Material 3 uses tonal surface elevation, not shadow depth
